output_freshness.curmudgeon: ~1 hour ago (WIN-056.c2.json at 17:07 UTC, tracker shows 55/67 Cycle 2 WINs reviewed)

handoff_issues.description: PERSISTENT P1 (3rd consecutive tinker report): digest-reviews.js processedWinIds fallback silently marks ALL Cycle 2 reviews as already-processed. 56 c2 review files on disk, processed-reviews ledger has 51 c2 entries, yet pending-digest.json shows 0 pending entries. The remaining 5 unprocessed c2 reviews (WIN-052 through WIN-056) are invisible to the decider. Additionally WIN-052.c2.json has a JSON parse error.
handoff_issues.evidence: pending-digest.json (generated 2026-04-07T17:54): total_reviews=67, already_processed=123, pending_count=0. The 123 'processed' count exceeds the 59 entries in the processed-reviews ledger — proving the processedWinIds fallback is double-counting by matching bare WIN IDs from Cycle 1 entries against Cycle 2 filenames. Decider daily-report-2026-04-07T16-30.json: curmudgeon_reviews_processed=[].

suggested_fix.rationale: The processedWinIds Set maps ALL processed filenames to bare WIN IDs (e.g., 'WIN-034.json' -> 'WIN-034'). When checking 'WIN-034.c2.json', entry.win_id='WIN-034' matches processedWinIds, so the Cycle 2 review is silently classified as already-processed. Removing this fallback is safe because: (1) processedSet already contains full filenames for both cycles, (2) any legacy bare-ID entries would still match via processedSet.has(file). This is the 3rd consecutive tinker report flagging this — it has been unfixed for >36 hours.

prompt_issues.description: Decider prompt at 414 lines — 65% over the 250-line ceiling. Flagged in 3 consecutive tinker reports. Growing instead of shrinking (was 339 lines when first flagged). Long prompts cause LLMs to deprioritize earlier instructions.

suggested_fix.rationale: Not self-fixable. Recommend human refactor: extract patch template schema (~50 lines), severity definitions (~30 lines), and expansion integration procedure (~60 lines) into monitor/prompts/decider-schemas.md, monitor/prompts/decider-severity.md, and monitor/prompts/decider-expansion-proc.md respectively. Decider reads these at runtime. Target: main prompt under 250 lines.

prompt_issues.description: Analyst prompt at 302 lines — 21% over the 250-line ceiling. Mode 3 (globe fingerprint hunt) procedure takes ~80 lines but is the lowest-priority mode.

suggested_fix.rationale: Not self-fixable. Extract Mode 3 procedure into monitor/prompts/analyst-mode3.md. Analyst reads it only when entering Mode 3.

prompt_issues.description: prose-extraction-task.md at 293 lines — 17% over ceiling. However this is a one-time task prompt, not a recurring agent. Lower concern.

suggested_fix.rationale: Low priority — this prompt is for a completed task and may not run again.

recommendations.description: URGENT (3rd escalation): Fix build-scripts/digest-reviews.js line 143-144. Remove processedWinIds fallback. Replace 3-way isProcessed check with `processedSet.has(file)` only. This restores the entire curmudgeon→decider data pipeline for Cycle 2 reviews.
recommendations.rationale: Cannot self-fix: build-scripts/ changes need a build cycle and are outside tinker's self-fix scope. Human must apply this fix.

recommendations.description: Investigate WIN-052.c2.json JSON parse error reported by digest-reviews.js. If the file is malformed, the curmudgeon will need to re-review WIN-052 in the next cycle, or the file needs manual repair.
Refactor decider.md (414 lines → target <250). Extract patch schema, severity matrix, and expansion integration into separate reference files read at runtime.
Extract analyst Mode 3 (globe fingerprint hunt) procedure into monitor/prompts/analyst-mode3.md to bring analyst.md under 250 lines.
Clear or archive monitor/curmudgeon/alerts.txt — current content documents WIN-005 issues from Cycle 1 that have been patched. Stale alerts cause alert fatigue.
recommendations.rationale: Low risk but deferring to next run — want to verify WIN-005 fixes are solid first.
